description:
You are a security-minded log analyst preparing an automated log-collection server for production.  
  Before you can deploy your collector, you must create a brand-new, single-purpose SSH key-pair and document its
  details in a tiny audit log so that the security team can later verify what was done.

  1. Generate a new Ed25519 key-pair with **NO pass-phrase** and comment “log_analyst@localhost”.
     • File names MUST be exactly  
       • private key : /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst  
       • public  key : /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst.pub  

  2. Append the **entire** public-key line (the one that starts with “ssh-ed25519 ”) to  
     /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys

  3. Obtain the SHA256 fingerprint of the **public** key in the canonical format produced by  
     `ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst.pub`  
     (it looks like “SHA256:xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx”).

  4. Create directory /home/user/ssh_setup if it does not already exist.

  5. Inside that directory create a single-line audit file called
     /home/user/ssh_setup/ssh_key_setup.log containing **exactly one line** following this pipe-delimited template:

        YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ|/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst|SHA256:…|N

     • Field 1 – UTC timestamp in strict ISO-8601 form (e.g. 2024-04-19T14:23:55Z).  
     • Field 2 – literal key-file path shown above (must match exactly).  
     • Field 3 – the SHA256 fingerprint obtained in step 3.  
     • Field 4 – the total number of keys currently present in /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys after you
       appended the new key (an integer, e.g. “1”, “2”, …).

     There must be **no leading/trailing spaces** and **exactly three (3) pipe characters** in the line.

  Success criteria (this is what the automated grader will check):
    • /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst and
      /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst.pub both exist and are valid Ed25519 keys.
    • The public key appears verbatim as its own line in /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys.
    • /home/user/ssh_setup/ssh_key_setup.log exists, contains one line conforming to the exact format above,
      and the third field’s fingerprint is the true SHA256 fingerprint of the new public key.
    • The fourth field equals the current line-count of /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys.

  Hints:
    • Work entirely as the normal “user” account; no root privileges are required.
    • Create directories with 700 permissions and new private keys with 600 permissions to satisfy OpenSSH defaults.
    • Use common command-line utilities (ssh-keygen, wc, date, etc.)—no need for exotic tools.
    • Feel free to echo intermediate information to the console; only the file locations and final log
      contents must match the specification above.
truth:
The automated test will:
    1. Verify existence and permissions of:
         • /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst             (regular file, mode 600)
         • /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst.pub         (regular file, mode 644)
         • /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys                   (regular file)
         • /home/user/ssh_setup/ssh_key_setup.log            (regular file)
    2. Parse /home/user/ssh_setup/ssh_key_setup.log:
         • Ensure it has exactly one line and three “|”.
         • Extract the timestamp (field1); validate ISO-8601 UTC format with trailing “Z”.
         • Confirm field2 equals “/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst”.
         • Compute SHA256 fingerprint of the public key with
              ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst.pub
           and ensure this matches field3 exactly.
         • Count non-blank lines in /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys
           and verify this integer equals field4.
    3. Confirm that the public-key line inside authorized_keys matches byte-for-byte the
       contents of /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst.pub.
    4. (Optionally) run `ssh-keygen -y -f /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_loganalyst` and compare the output
       to the public-key line to assure the key-pair is consistent.
name: task_000000_7cd2e4d9
